phenomenon: M87 Black Hole Shadow -- First Direct Image (Event Horizon Telescope, 2019)
key_measurement: Black hole mass: 6.5 +/- 0.7 billion solar masses; shadow angular diameter ~42 +/- 3 microarcseconds (Event Horizon Telescope Collaboration, ApJL 875:L1, 2019)

sources.citation: Event Horizon Telescope Collaboration et al., First M87 Event Horizon Telescope Results. I. The Shadow of the Supermassive Black Hole, ApJL 875, L1 (2019)
Event Horizon Telescope Collaboration et al., First M87 Event Horizon Telescope Results. VI. The Shadow and Mass of the Central Black Hole, ApJL 875, L6 (2019)
Event Horizon Telescope Collaboration et al., First M87 Event Horizon Telescope Results. V. Physical Origin of the Asymmetric Ring, ApJL 875, L5 (2019)
NASA -- Black Holes: Science overview including M87* shadow and supermassive black hole demographics
National Science Foundation -- EHT Press Release: Astronomers Capture First Image of a Black Hole (April 10, 2019)

visualization_compliance.shadow_diameter_uas: 42 +/- 3 microarcseconds -- do not render smaller or rounder than EHT constraint
visualization_compliance.photon_ring: Bright asymmetric ring required; southern limb brighter due to Doppler beaming from approaching jet side
visualization_compliance.black_hole_interior: No luminous black hole surface -- shadow only; event horizon is NOT a physical surface
visualization_compliance.accretion_disk: Accretion disk temperature gradient required; disk inclined ~17 degrees to line of sight
visualization_compliance.doppler_beaming: Doppler beaming on inclined disk must create brightness asymmetry
visualization_compliance.distance_label: Distance: 16.8 Mpc (~55 million light-years), Virgo A galaxy (M87)
visualization_compliance.labels_required: ILLUSTRATIVE + NOT TO SCALE labels on payoff frame; NOT EHT DATA unless using actual interferometry pixels

eht_specifics.relativistic_jet: ~5,000 light-year relativistic jet confirmed in radio/optical
